job_profiles.label: Product Planning
job_profiles.question: Which workflow and skills should turn a product idea into a testable, accepted delivery plan?

workflow_routes.when: A product or opportunity starts from discovery and must continue through release and learning.
The opportunity is accepted and needs a precise specification before implementation.

capability_groups.purpose: Validate value and the riskiest assumptions
Define requirements, release units, and delivery topology

expected_evidence: Accepted problem framing and target user evidence.
Explicit value hypothesis, assumptions, risks, and decision rule.
PRD or equivalent requirements with measurable acceptance criteria.
Release-unit decomposition with dependencies, branch base, PR target, and product acceptance owner.
A clear stop, experiment, specify, or implementation decision.
job_profiles.completion_gate: Planning is complete only when the opportunity is accepted or stopped, the next release unit has testable requirements and ownership, and implementation is not being used to resolve an untested product assumption.

job_profiles.label: Engineering Quality
job_profiles.question: Which workflows and skills should produce maintainable, well-tested, review-ready code?

workflow_routes.when: Build an approved capability in an existing product.
Repair behavior that already exists but is broken or regressed.

capability_groups.purpose: Route, own, and map repository constraints
Design and implement the technical solution
Verify quality through independent review and correct causal findings

expected_evidence: Verified repository conventions and an explicit reuse, extension, composition, native, or dependency decision.
Tests that prove the accepted behavior and regression boundary.
Internal code-quality evidence with named findings, behavior-preservation risk, and materially applicable SOLID or architecture-style decisions.
Implementation evidence tied to the accepted requirements and architecture boundary.
Architecture, security, and code-review findings with severity and reproducibility.
A clear merge-ready, needs-work, or blocked verdict.
job_profiles.completion_gate: Engineering work is complete only when accepted behavior is implemented, focused tests and applicable quality gates pass, material findings are resolved or explicitly blocked, and merge authorization remains distinct from implementation completion.

job_profiles.label: Security Engineering
job_profiles.question: Which workflows and skills should design, implement, review, and operate secure product behavior?

workflow_routes.when: Add or materially change a security-sensitive capability.
Review a candidate change or existing implementation for security risk.
Repair a confirmed security regression or vulnerability.
Release a security-sensitive change and verify runtime health.

capability_groups.purpose: Establish authority, assets, boundaries, and threat assumptions
Verify independently, release safely, observe, and respond

expected_evidence: Protected assets, actors, trust boundaries, data flows, and abuse cases.
Threats ranked by likelihood and impact with explicit mitigations and owners.
Security requirements represented in architecture, API, data, authentication, authorization, and failure behavior.
Reproducible security-review findings and verification of the affected boundary.
Release, observability, rollback, and incident ownership for material residual risk.
job_profiles.completion_gate: Security work is complete only when material threats have verified mitigations or explicit accepted ownership, security review has a clear verdict, and release or operational risk is not inferred from code completion alone.
